question: Is the anime girl clearly recognizable as the character from the first input image (silver/lavender hair, purple/pink dress, star accessories)?
Does the girl retain her 2D anime art style (cel-shaded look, illustrative features) within the composite?
Is the green rhinoceros beetle clearly recognizable with its distinctive horn and metallic shell texture?
Does the beetle retain its photorealistic macro photography style (realistic lighting, high detail)?
Is the anime girl positioned specifically on the back of the rhinoceros beetle (elytra or pronotum area)?
Has the anime girl been scaled down significantly to appear as a tiny rider fitting proportionally on the insect?
Does the girl's pose suggest she is riding or sitting on the beetle (straddling or resting weight) rather than standing or floating unrelatedly?
Is the background consistent with the macro photography environment (green leaf, blurred forest bokeh) of the beetle image?
Are there visible contact shadows cast by the girl onto the beetle's back to ground her physically in the scene?
Does the lighting direction and intensity on the anime girl match the lighting environment of the beetle photo?
Are the edges of the anime girl clean and well-blended, without white halos or jagged 'cutout' artifacts?
Is the depth of field consistent, ensuring the girl shares similar focus characteristics as the part of the beetle she is sitting on?
Is the anime girl's anatomy coherent (no missing limbs, extra hands, or severe facial distortions)?
Is the beetle's anatomy structurally correct (no missing legs, deformed shell, or duplicate horns)?
Is the image free from severe hallucinations (e.g., multiple girls, multiple beetles, or unrelated objects)?
